adventure.explanation: Peak District's rugged beauty and iconic climbs deliver pure cycling adventure on paved roads.

altitude.explanation: Moderate highs around Whetstone Ridge, but not extreme altitude challenges.

climate.explanation: Spring weather in May can be variable, with rain risks but mild temps.

community.explanation: Strong UK sportive scene with finisher medals and local vibes in Bakewell.

elevation.explanation: Nearly 10,000ft of brutal Peak District climbing makes legs scream.

expenses.explanation: Affordable £40-45 entry with great value feeds and support.

experience.explanation: Historic route with pro pedigrees; a must-ride classic.

field_depth.explanation: Around 2,500 riders participate annually, mixing club cyclists with serious sportive riders. Not a UCI qualifier but draws strong regional competition from across the Midlands and North England.

length.explanation: 113-mile long route tests endurance without excess.

logistics.explanation: Well-organized with timed slots, feeds, and Bakewell HQ.

race_quality.explanation: Fully supported sportive with chip timing and mechanical aid.

technicality.explanation: Steep, savage climbs like Winnats Pass demand bike handling skills.

value.explanation: Epic climbing for low cost; unbeatable bang for buck.

cultural_impact.explanation: Follows legendary 1943-2005 race tracks of British cycling heroes.

prestige.explanation: Long-running Peak District sportive with timed Winnats Pass section, established in UK cycling calendar.

course_description.character: Brutal road sportive through Peak District National Park, relentless hills with savage short pitches and long grinds, rewarded by stunning vistas and fast descents.

suffering_zones.label: Winnats Pass
suffering_zones.description: Killer steep ramp early on, splitting medium from long routes; unrelenting gradient tests resolve.

suffering_zones.label: Holme Moss
suffering_zones.description: Long, enduring climb on long route; high-altitude grind after Winnats.

suffering_zones.label: Cat and Fiddle
suffering_zones.description: Final major test before Bakewell; iconic high road with wind exposure.

suffering_zones.label: Whetstone Ridge
suffering_zones.description: Long route high point; cumulative fatigue from 9500ft total ascent hits here.

course_description.signature_challenge: The 'ride of heroes' long route tackling Winnats Pass, Holme Moss, and Cat and Fiddle in one day—over 10,000ft of Peak District punishment.

logistics.overview: Bakewell Showground HQ with timed departures 06:30-07:30, pre-event questionnaire required, no day entries, feeds (1-3), chip timing, medical/mechanical support, finisher medal.
logistics.accommodation: Local Bakewell options; try traditional Bakewell Pudding post-ride.
logistics.getting_there: Drive or train to Bakewell, Derbyshire; showground central for Peak District access.
logistics.spectator_info: Start/finish at showground; best views on major climbs like Winnats Pass.

climate.overview: Typical Peak District spring: mild but changeable with rain and wind common.
climate.wind_conditions: Exposed ridges bring crosswinds; climbs often into headwinds.
climate.altitude_effects: Minimal hypoxia, but cooler temps at 1500-2000ft peaks.

terrain.terrain_overview: 100% paved roads through Peak District; mix of narrow lanes, open moors, and valley rollers.
terrain.key_features: Short savage climbs (Mam Tor, Winnats), long hauls (Holme Moss, Cat and Fiddle), high point Whetstone Ridge; 113 miles, 9500ft gain.

history.overview: Sportive revives the classic Tour of the Peak road race (1943-2005), wheel tracks of British legends like Malcolm Elliott.
history.notable_editions: Original race hosted top pros; modern sportive upholds tough legacy with multiple distances.
history.growth_trajectory: Established annual event with consistent routes; Velo29 organizing for years, building rider loyalty.

final_verdict.one_liner: Peak District's savage climbs on historic roads—a true test of grit.
final_verdict.best_for: Climbers seeking UK classics with massive vert and scenery.
final_verdict.pro_tip: Train steep ramps; pack rain gear for May; fuel heavy for 9500ft.

citations.title: Tour of the Peak Sportive
citations.snippet: This classic sportive, named after and follows the same route as the original Tour of the Peak road race which ran from 1943 through to 2005.

citations.title: The Peaks Tour | 2026 | Cycling Races in Derbyshire
citations.snippet: The Peaks Tour 2025 is a challenging cyclosportive through the scenic Peak District, starting and finishing at Bakewell Showground.

citations.title: The UKCE Peaks Tour 2026 | Sat 5 Sept | Book @ Findarace
citations.snippet: On the Long Route, you'll hit the high point at Whetstone Ridge, but with other climbs including Mam Tor, Gun and Sir William's Hill along the way.

citations.title: The Peaks Tour | Long | Sat 27 Sep 2025 - TimeOutdoors
citations.snippet: The Peaks Tour sportive takes riders on an adventure through the Peak District National Park.
